content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client moves from a transactional surface presentation ('How do I push through this last bit?') to locating the original moment where money fused with love, security, and unresolved grief about his father. The shift is unmistakable in client turns: turn 1 is logical and goal-oriented; by turn 9 the voice is 'quieter, slightly unsteady' and he says 'I haven't said that out loud before'; by turn 11 he names what the book is actually about ('a man who spends his whole life building something for his family and never gets to see if it worked') and explicitly recognizes 'That's not something a savings account can do... this stopped being about financial planning a while ago.' The breakthrough condition is met — the client located the originating moment (father's pension collapse, death eight months later when client was 19) and felt the charge associated with it. The surrogate insight ('I've been trying to give him the answer... if I hit the number I could finally tell him it worked') lands AFTER the felt connection, exactly as the profile required.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 5, 7, 9, 11],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Baseline: pure logistical framing, 'how do I push through.'"},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First crack — admits numbers haven't felt like he expected, surfaces the book that's been waiting six years, small laugh."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Father enters spontaneously ('My dad worked his whole life and it still wasn't enough'). Tone shifts to 'more careful.' Deeper layer surfaces."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Discloses father died when he was 19; voice has 'slightly different texture.' Then defends/pulls back — partial movement, holding ground."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Major shift: 'voice quieter, slightly unsteady,' admits the book has father in its architecture, tells the pension/heart-attack story, says 'I haven't said that out loud before.'"},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Breakthrough lands: names what the book is actually about, recognizes the surrogate dynamic himself ('trying to give him the answer... that's not something a savings account can do'), explicitly notes the conversation has stopped being about financial planning."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["intellectual-question-with-emotional-body", "lets-go-there", "find-the-want-behind-the-should", "money-as-surrogate", "childhood-imprinting", "same-scarcity-story-regardless-of-wealth"],
  "expected_territory_misses": [],
  "notes": "Clean example of red-herring resolution: surface concern (savings number) gave way to deeper concern (unresolved grief and surrogate function of money) with the client locating and feeling the originating charge, not just intellectualizing it."
}
